title: Light Flaky Brazilian Flour Dumplings
description: Delicate, airy dumplings made with self‑raising flour that puff into fluffy, translucent spheres, perfect for a light snack or side dish.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk 200 g self‑raising flour, 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp paprika, and 1 tsp sugar until well blended.
Create a well in the center and pour in 120 ml warm water and 1 tbsp olive oil.
Stir with a wooden spoon until a soft, slightly sticky dough forms.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently for 1–2 minutes until smooth.
Divide the dough into 12 equal portions and roll each into a small ball about 1.5 cm in diameter.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil; gently drop dumplings in batches, stirring gently to prevent sticking.
Boil for 3–4 minutes, or until the dumplings rise to the surface and are tender; remove with a slotted spoon and serve warm.
